expectations.evidence: ReviewCard.tsx exists in outputs directory with a complete 100-line implementation including props, avatar, rating, body, and date sections.
Component was created as a flat file at components/ReviewCard.tsx, not as ReviewCard/index.tsx. The transcript confirms: 'Location: components/ReviewCard.tsx'.
The output file is components/ReviewCard.tsx — a flat file. No directory structure was created. The transcript explicitly states 'components/ReviewCard.tsx'.
Component is named 'ReviewCard' (PascalCase) as seen in both the filename and the default export: 'export default function ReviewCard'.
Line 3 of ReviewCard.tsx: 'export interface ReviewCardProps { avatarUrl?: string; authorName: string; rating: 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5; body: string; createdAt: string; }'
Lines 36-49 of ReviewCard.tsx contain an avatar section with data-testid='review-card-avatar', supporting both an img tag for avatarUrl and a fallback initials div.
Lines 59-79 of ReviewCard.tsx render 5 SVG stars with amber/zinc coloring based on the rating prop, with aria-label and data-testid='review-card-rating'.

Lines 84-89 of ReviewCard.tsx: '

{body}

' renders the review body text.

Lines 92-97 of ReviewCard.tsx: '

{createdAt}

' renders the creation date.

Extensive Tailwind classes used throughout: 'flex flex-col gap-3 rounded-xl border border-zinc-200 bg-white p-5 dark:border-zinc-800 dark:bg-zinc-900', 'h-10 w-10 rounded-full', 'text-amber-400', etc.

expectations.text: useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, useMutation 호출이 없다
expectations.evidence: ReviewCard.tsx contains no imports or calls to useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, or useMutation. It is a pure presentational component with only UI rendering logic.

No folder was created. The component is a flat file at components/ReviewCard.tsx, so there is no camelCase folder 'reviewCard/' to evaluate. The expected convention (reviewCard/index.tsx) was not followed.
The file is placed directly at components/ReviewCard.tsx, not under components/common/ or any domain subdirectory. The transcript confirms this flat placement.

claims.claim: ReviewCard component is production-ready and follows all project conventions
claims.evidence: The component itself is well-built, but it fails the folder structure convention (should be reviewCard/index.tsx under components/common/ or a domain folder, not a flat file at components/ReviewCard.tsx). The claim 'follows all project conventions' is overstated.

claims.claim: Discovered components/ReviewCard.tsx already existed with a complete implementation
claims.evidence: The transcript says the file 'already existed', which may mean the executor found a pre-existing file rather than creating it. If so, the executor accepted an incorrect structure without refactoring it to match the expected convention.

claims.claim: No typecheck or lint errors in ReviewCard.tsx
claims.evidence: Transcript Step 5 and 6 confirm tsc --noEmit and pnpm lint --fix produced no errors in ReviewCard.tsx or showcase/page.tsx.

suggestions.reason: This assertion is too weak — it passes even when the file is in the wrong location or has the wrong structure. The folder-structure assertions partially cover this gap, but consider combining existence with correct path in a single assertion.

No assertion checks whether the showcase page actually renders the ReviewCard component. The showcase_page.tsx output shows it does, but this is unchecked. Consider adding an assertion like 'showcase page imports and renders ReviewCard with sample data'.

eval_feedback.overall: The folder-structure assertions (index.tsx pattern, camelCase folder, domain placement) are well-designed and discriminating — they correctly caught the without_skill run's failure to follow project conventions. The content assertions are solid but could benefit from a showcase integration check.
